title: Rich Korean White Sausage Jeon
description: Jammy, molten, comforting pancake featuring vegetarian white sausage and pickled daikon.
instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and lightly coat with sesame oil.
While the skillet warms, whisk together flour, water, egg, salt, and sugar in a bowl until smooth.
Stir in minced scallions and sliced nuka-pickle into the batter, mixing gently to avoid breaking the pickles.
Slice each vegetarian white sausage into 1/4 inch thick rounds.
Drop spoonfuls of batter onto the skillet, placing sausage slices on top and allowing the edges to bubble.
Cook each jeon for 3 minutes until the bottom turns golden brown and the batter is firm.
Flip carefully with a spatula and cook for another 2 minutes until the other side is crisp.
Transfer the finished jeon to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the hot jeon for added flavor.
Serve immediately with a side of soy sauce or a small bowl of melted white sauce.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
